India–Egypt Boost Defence Cooperation

India and Egypt have agreed on a bilateral defence cooperation plan for 2026–27 following the 11th Joint Defence Committee meeting in Cairo.

Focus areas:
1️⃣ Expanded joint military training & exercises
2️⃣ Enhanced maritime security cooperation
3️⃣ Greater defence industry collaboration (co-development & co-production)
4️⃣ Stronger structured military engagement mechanisms

The two sides also held Navy-to-Navy staff talks, highlighting cooperation in the Indian Ocean and maritime security frameworks.

RT by @sidhant: As the son of immigrants from India, I take great pride in both my heritage and in the country that gave my family the opportunity to build a better life. My parents came to the United States legally in search of that opportunity. 

My mother spent 35 years working as a public school teacher. My father worked as an engineer. They instilled in my brothers and me a deep belief in hard work, public service, and giving back to the country that welcomed them.

I am a product of that American story. I attended California’s public schools from kindergarten through medical school, became a doctor, and now have the privilege of serving our nation in Congress. That is what the American Dream looks like.

BREAKING: Iran Refuses to Reopen Strait of Hormuz

Iran says it will not reopen the Strait of Hormuz as long as a U.S. naval blockade remains in place, despite the ongoing ceasefire.

Tehran has also seized two vessels attempting to transit the route, signaling continued enforcement of its position.

Iranian official Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf stated:
“A ceasefire has no meaning if it is violated through a naval blockade.”

India and Germany are close to sealing a $8 billion submarine deal, expected to be signed within the next three months, according to German Defence Minister Boris Pistorius after talks with Rajnath Singh.

Details:
- 6 submarines to be built in India.
- Collaboration between Thyssenkrupp Marine Systems (TKMS) and Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited.
- Includes technology transfer to India; a first for Germany outside Europe.

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief: 
22nd April 2026  

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East 
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief.  

● Trump announced an indefinite extension of the ceasefire, citing the need for a unified proposal from Iran.

● IRGC forces fired on commercial vessels in/near the Strait of Hormuz due to maritime violations.

● Two vessels (Epaminondas and MSC Francesca) were seized and taken to Iranian waters; a third vessel (Euphoria) was damaged.

● Hezbollah launched a hostile aircraft toward IDF soldiers operating south of the Forward Defense Line, in the area of southern Lebanon.

● The IDF eliminated Khamis Muhammad Khamis Qassas, who infiltrated Kibbutz Nir Oz during the Oct. 7 Massacre. He recently led terrorists to the Yellow Line area.

● Iran has not issued any official statement confirming its agreement to extend the ceasefire, despite unverified reports circulating in some media outlets.

● The IDF carried out a targeted strike in the Saluki stream area, eliminating two Hezbollah militants who allegedly crossed the security strip and approached Israeli forces.

● Iran's ambassador Amir Saeid Iravani urged the Security Council to compel the US to release the vessel TOUSKA, its crew, their families and all affected persons without condition.

END IF THE BRIEF
